title: Questions for Patrick Mooney — VC informational interview
html:
TL;DR

  • April 12, 2023 prep doc — 7 questions Yahir drafted for an informational with Patrick Mooney, an ESG-flavored VC tied to Lemelson Foundation, Potencia Ventures (education-focused), and Craft3 (community lender).
  • Question themes: why ESG specifically? Are education ventures profitable or values-driven? Opinion on UBI? Catalyst for going independent? Internal vs external motivation? How stingy with founder advice when no equity? Is Craft3 using DeFi to bypass banks?
  • The Craft3-DeFi question is the giveaway: Yahir is fishing for an ESG VC who'd entertain his crypto defi worldview — early signal of the Web3 era thinking that crystallizes the same year.
  • Sits in the same Q1-2023 OSU junior year networking push as ELI and the Occam connection cycle — Yahir is actively building an adult-mentor network around money/values intersections.

Notes

The questions are well-crafted — internal-vs-external-motivation is a classic "reveal yourself" prompt, and the "how stingy are you with advice when you have no equity" question is a mentor-quality test. The Craft3-DeFi question shows Yahir is already triangulating the same theme that becomes fweb3 later: traditional community lending vs. on-chain disintermediation.

Lemelson Foundation is Oregon-flavored (impact investing for invention/entrepreneurship) — likely how Yahir found Mooney via the Ford / Oregon adjacency network. Outcome of the meeting is not in this doc — flag for later densify if a follow-up surfaces.

Connections

  • patrick mooney — the subject
  • ubi · crypto · web3 era — the ideologies Yahir is testing on him
  • — the genre of relationship being built
  • ford family foundation · lemelson foundation — the Oregon impact-investing network
  • 2023 01 30 eli internship — same-quarter networking push
